batches.theme: Local-sensitive urban and outdoor detail visuals
batches.rationale: First batch for locality-sensitive prompts with strong no-fake-place constraints.

requests.prompt: Create one premium AI-generated symbolic event visual for the "Bocholt erleben" PWA. This is a landscape event-card image and must be optimized for a 4:3 crop-safe composition. Visual key: city_festival. Planned asset path: /assets/event-visuals/city-festival-01.webp. Image class: local_sensitive_detail. Locally plausible detail or atmosphere imagery for visual keys where users may notice fake place claims. Subject focus: urban festive small-town atmosphere through details, planters, temporary setup cues, tables, light decor, paving, facades. Specific slot brief: Bocholter oder ähnlich kleinstädtische Fußgängerzone mit Event-/Fest-Atmosphäre.. Locality requirement: must feel locally plausible and must avoid invented central squares. Anchor style profiles to follow: city_walk_v2, market_food_v3. Emphasize these anchor strengths: locally plausible small-town mood; brick, plaster, paving and plant materiality; calm editorial realism; no false concrete place claim; strong card crop suitability; event-context food imagery instead of pure food styling; market stand materiality; soft anonymous background context; warm but believable editorial look; card-friendly subject layering. Do not transfer these anchor weaknesses: over-clean perfection; postcard romanticism; too much visual emptiness; gourmet magazine still life; alcohol-centric mood; perfect fantasy market square. Desired visual language: premium editorial photography; believable real-world realism; warm-neutral restrained palette; quiet premium composition; material-rich surfaces; slight lived-in imperfection; calm local small-town atmosphere where relevant. Lighting rules: prefer natural daylight or soft diffused light; allow mild warmth but avoid heavy golden-hour glaze; allow gentle indoor softness for symbolic scenes; avoid extreme cinematic light beams or fantasy haze. Composition rules: landscape image optimized for 4:3 crop safety; clear foreground or midground subject focus; background may be soft but not unrealistically over-blurred; avoid overcrowding and avoid too many micro-details; preserve usable crop for mobile cards. Realism rules: surfaces should feel slightly imperfect and tactile; avoid plastic-looking textures; avoid impossible geometry or suspicious repetition; avoid theme-park prettiness; prefer documentary editorial restraint over visual spectacle. Class-specific prompt bias: subtle small-town detail; brick, plaster, paving, greenery, bicycle or street furniture; editorial documentary realism. Must include: detail or edge framing instead of a full square; small-town urban materials; subtle event cues. People policy: anonymous or absent. Prompt-specific avoid rules: big fantasy festival plaza; tourism postcard composition; generic big-city street festival. Class-specific avoid bias: full postcard townscape; romanticized fantasy old town; tourism brochure angle. Global negative rules: no fictional recognizable old-town square; no postcard old-town look; no fairy-tale half-timber fantasy street; no over-perfect golden-hour postcard mood; no cheap stock-photo smile scenes; no exaggerated cinematic bokeh; no surreal AI symmetry; no pristine showroom perfection; no loud festival poster aesthetics; no text in image. Legal safety rules: no readable brands; no readable posters; no readable signs; no copyrighted artwork in focus; no identifiable children; avoid identifiable adults; if present, keep anonymous or partial. Do not include any readable text, logos, posters, signs or copyrighted artwork. Do not create a false concrete Bocholt place claim. The image must feel premium, believable, low in AI-artifice, and suitable for a modern discovery PWA event card.

metadata.notes: Für Premium-Wirkung möglichst lokal. Generische Stadtfestbilder nur als Notlösung.
